title: Mastering font-display for Superior Web Font Loading and User Experience
tweet: Boost your website's perceived performance and enhance user experience! Learn how to effectively use `font-display` in your `@font-face` rules to control web font loading behavior and prevent layout shifts (CLS). #CSSTypography #WebFonts #UIDesign #UX
tip: Always include a `font-display` descriptor in your `@font-face` rules. For most body text, `font-display: swap;` offers the best balance, showing a fallback quickly and swapping in your custom font when ready. For critical hero text, `block` might be acceptable, but for secondary elements, consider `fallback` or `optional` to minimize layout shifts (CLS) and ensure a fast initial render, prioritizing readability.
